verses.commentary: God has not rejected Israel. Paul himself is proof: an Israelite, a believer in Christ. God's promises to Israel still stand.
Foreknew implies electing love, not mere prescience. God knew and chose Israel. The Elijah example will illustrate the remnant principle.
Elijah's despair: I alone am left. He thought faithfulness had disappeared. This is the cry of a discouraged prophet who sees only apostasy.
God's response: You are not alone. Seven thousand remained faithful. God always preserves a remnant. Faithfulness is never as rare as it seems. God keeps His own.
A remnant of Israel believes in Christ. Chosen by grace emphasizes divine election, not human merit. Grace, not ethnicity, defines the remnant.
Adding works to grace destroys grace. Salvation is entirely by grace or not at all. This is a foundational principle.
The others were hardened (pōroō, πωρόω, made dull, insensitive). God's judicial hardening is a response to persistent unbelief.
Judicial blindness: God gave them spiritual stupor. They cannot see or hear spiritually. This is God's judgment on unbelief. To this very day shows it continues.
David's imprecation is applied to Israel. What should bless (the table) becomes a curse. Blessing rejected becomes judgment. This is the irony of hardening.
Darkened eyes and bent backs depict spiritual blindness and burden. Those who reject light are given over to darkness. This is both judgment and mercy withheld.
Their rejection opened the door for Gentile salvation. God's purpose: to provoke Israel to jealousy, moving them to embrace Christ. Israel's fall is not final.
Israel's full acceptance will bring even greater blessing. Their full inclusion (plērōma, πλήρωμα, fullness) suggests future restoration.
Paul addresses Gentiles directly. His role as apostle to the Gentiles is a source of pride (not arrogance but joy in calling). His Gentile mission serves a larger purpose.
If Israel sees Gentiles enjoying covenant blessings, they may seek Christ. Save some acknowledges not all will respond, but some will.

Rejection brought reconciliation (the gospel went to Gentiles). Acceptance will bring life from the dead. This likely refers to resurrection and the age to come. Israel's restoration signals the end times.
Firstfruits (patriarchs) sanctify the whole (Israel). Root (Abraham and the patriarchs) sanctifies branches (descendants). Israel retains a holy status because of God's covenant with the fathers.
Some natural branches (unbelieving Jews) were broken off. Wild olive shoots (Gentiles) were grafted in. Gentiles now share in covenant blessings (the nourishing sap).
Gentiles are grafted in; they do not replace Israel. The root (patriarchs, covenant) supports the branches, not vice versa. Gentiles owe everything to Israel's heritage.
This misunderstands God's purposes. Israel's rejection was not for Gentile superiority but for global salvation.
Gentiles stand by faith, not superior merit. Arrogance is dangerous. Tremble (phobou, φοβοῦ, fear) is a call to humble reverence. Pride precedes a fall.
Natural branches (ethnic Israel) were not exempt. Wild branches (Gentiles) will not be either. God's justice is impartial.
Sternness fell on unbelieving Israel. Kindness is shown to believing Gentiles, but only if they continue in faith. Apostasy results in being cut off. This is a warning against presumption.
If they abandon unbelief, they will be re-grafted. God is able emphasizes divine power. What seems impossible (restoring Israel) is possible with God.

Re-grafting Jews (natural) is according to nature. If God did the harder thing (grafting in Gentiles), He will certainly do the easier thing (restoring Israel). Israel's restoration is more natural than Gentile inclusion.

Israel's hardening is partial (not all) and temporary (until). The full number of Gentiles refers to the completion of Gentile salvation. Purpose: to prevent Gentile conceit. Gentiles should not despise Israel; God's plan includes both.

All Israel does not mean every individual Jew but the nation corporately, or the full number of elect Jews. In this way suggests a process: after Gentile fullness, Israel's salvation. The Deliverer is Christ. He will return and turn Israel from godlessness. This is future, eschatological.
God's covenant promise: sins will be removed. This is the new covenant. Israel's future includes full forgiveness and restoration. God's covenant faithfulness ensures it.
the patriarchs). Israel is both judged and loved. God's election is irrevocable.
ns without repentance, unchangeable. God does not revoke His gifts (covenant blessings) or call (election of Israel). This guarantees Israel's future restoration. God's promises stand forever.
Israel's rejection became the occasion for Gentile salvation. God's sovereignty orchestrates even rebellion for merciful purposes.
God's mercy to Gentiles provokes Israel to jealousy, leading to their salvation. Mercy begets mercy. God's plan encompasses all.
God's purpose: to have mercy on all (both groups). All are guilty so all must receive mercy. This magnifies grace. Salvation is entirely of mercy, not merit.

God's riches, wisdom, and knowledge are unfathomable. His judgments (krisis, κρίσις, decisions) are unsearchable. His paths (hodos, ὁδός, ways) are untraceable. We cannot fully comprehend God's plans. Humble awe is the right response.
Rhetorical questions expecting no. No one knows God's mind fully. No one advises God. He needs no counsel. This underscores divine sovereignty and human finitude.
No one can put God in debt. God owes nothing to anyone. All good comes from His grace, not our merit. We are recipients, not creditors.
ll things. All things exist because of God, are sustained by God, and exist for God's glory. To Him be the glory forever captures the purpose of all existence. Amen (so be it) seals the doxology.
